Name: Tangela
Flavor-Text: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

Name: Tangrowth
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases. Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.

Name: Pinsir
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. At the end of your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

Name: Spinarak
Flavor-Text: Some fishers weave its sturdy thread into nets to catch fish Pokémon.

Name: Ariados
Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Active Evolution Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C] more.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It wanders in search of food after darkness falls, never nesting in a specific place.

Name: Sunkern
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is very weak. Its only means of defense is to shake its leaves desperately at its attacker.

Name: Sunflora
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each [R] Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Since it converts sunlight into energy, it is always looking in the direction of the sun.

Name: Heracross
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: With its herculean powers, it can easily throw around an object that is 100 times its own weight.

Name: Volbeat
Effects: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
If Illumise is on your Bench, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It flashes the light on its rear to communicate with other Volbeat. It loves the sweet aroma given off by Illumise.

Name: Illumise
Effects: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Shuffle 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It guides Volbeat to draw signs in the night sky. There are scholars who research the meaning of these signs.

Name: Leafeon
Effects: Attach a Basic [G] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal all damage from that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The younger they are, the more they smell like fresh grass. With age, their fragrance takes on the odor of fallen leaves.

Name: Phantump
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon came to be when the spirit of a deceased child possessed a tree stump. It now wanders the forest searching for friends.

Name: Trevenant
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Trevenant is very kind to Pokémon living in the forest. It doesn’t even care if these Pokémon take up residence in the greenery on its head.

Name: Grookey
Flavor-Text: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

Name: Thwackey
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if your Active Pokémon has the Festival Lead Ability, you may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

Name: Rillaboom
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost [C] more, and its Retreat Cost is [C] more.
This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

Name: Applin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.

Name: Dipplin
Ability-Effect: If Festival Grounds is in play, this Pokémon may use an attack it has twice. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.

Name: Iron Leaves
Effects: Put up to 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: According to the few eyewitness accounts that exist, it used its shining blades to julienne large trees and boulders.

Name: Poltchageist
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Poltchageist looks like a regional form of Sinistea, but it was recently discovered that the two Pokémon are entirely unrelated.

Name: Poltchageist
Effects: Put a Basic [G] Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Poltchageist looks like a regional form of Sinistea, but it was recently discovered that the two Pokémon are entirely unrelated.

Name: Sinistcha
Effects: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Discard up to 3 [G] Energy cards from your Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: It prefers cool, dark places, such as the back of a shelf or the space beneath a home’s floorboards. It wanders in search of prey after sunset.

Name: Sinistcha ex
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each Basic [G] Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those Energy cards into your deck.
Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Teal Mask Ogerpon
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This mischief-loving Pokémon is full of curiosity. It battles by drawing out the type-based energy contained within its masks.

Name: Teal Mask Ogerpon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [G] Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Vulpix
Flavor-Text: As its body grows larger, its six warm tails become more beautiful, with a more luxurious coat of fur.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.
Flavor-Text: It has nine long tails and fur that gleams gold. It is said to live for 1,000 years.

Name: Slugma
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that in ancient times, when volcanoes were more active, there were many more Slugma than there are now.

Name: Magcargo ex
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard the top card of each player’s deck. This attack does 140 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Torkoal
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Coal is the source of Torkoal’s energy. Large amounts of coal can be found in the mountains where they live.

Name: Chimchar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its fiery rear end is fueled by gas made in its belly. Even rain can’t extinguish the fire.

Name: Monferno
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It skillfully controls the intensity of the fire on its tail to keep its foes at an ideal distance.

Name: Infernape
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [R] Energy card, a Basic [F] Energy card, or 1 of each from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its crown of fire is indicative of its fiery nature. It is beaten by none in terms of quickness.

Name: Darumaka
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It derives its power from fire burning inside its body. If the fire dwindles, this Pokémon will immediately fall asleep.

Name: Darmanitan
Effects: This Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The thick arms of this hot-blooded Pokémon can deliver punches capable of obliterating a dump truck.

Name: Litwick
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life-force of any who follow it.

Name: Lampent
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

Name: Chandelure
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may have each player draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each card in your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs spirits, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

Name: Chi-Yu
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 60 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.
Flavor-Text: The envy accumulated within curved beads that sparked multiple conflicts has clad itself in fire and become a Pokémon.

Name: Hearthflame Mask Ogerpon ex
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage, and discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Poliwag
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its legs are newly grown, and it can’t walk very well. It seems to prefer swimming through the water instead.

Name: Poliwhirl
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its skin is moist all over its body. The skin on its belly spiral also feels smooth.

Name: Poliwrath
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
You may do 120 more damage. If you do, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: It can use its well-developed arms and legs to run on the surface of the water for a split second.

Name: Goldeen
Ability-Effect: If Festival Grounds is in play, this Pokémon may use an attack it has twice. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the Water Dancer.

Name: Seaking
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

Name: Jynx
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. If you put any Pokémon onto your Bench in this way, move an Energy from this Pokémon to the new Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In certain parts of Galar, Jynx was once feared and worshiped as the Queen of Ice.

Name: Corphish
Flavor-Text: It can live in impure water, where it doesn’t need to compete with other water Pokémon for food, so its numbers have steadily increased.

Name: Crawdaunt
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: A rough customer that wildly flails its giant claws. It is said to be extremely hard to raise.

Name: Feebas
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is the shabbiest Pokémon of all. It forms schools and lives at the bottom of rivers that teem with plant life.

Name: Milotic
Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Pokémon in play and all attached cards can’t be put into your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It lives at the bottom of clear lakes. In times of war, it shows itself, which soothes people’s minds and hearts.

Name: Snorunt
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It is said that a home visited by a Snorunt will prosper. It can withstand cold of −150 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Glalie
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It prevents prey from escaping by instantaneously freezing moisture in the air.

Name: Froslass
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, put 1 damage counter on each Pokémon that has an Ability (both yours and your opponent’s), except any Froslass.
Flavor-Text: It freezes prey by blowing its −58 degree Fahrenheit breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.

Name: Glaceon
Effects: At the end of your opponent’s next turn, put 9 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lowers its body heat to freeze its fur. The hairs then become like needles it can fire.

Name: Phione
Effects: Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the water warms, they inflate the flotation sac on their heads and drift languidly on the sea in packs.

Name: Froakie
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Froakie and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

Name: Frogadier
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

Name: Cramorant
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It’s an incredibly poor learner because it uses the bare minimum amount of energy for its brain. It focuses on battling instead.

Name: Finizen
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Its water ring is made from seawater mixed with a sticky fluid that Finizen secretes from its blowhole.

Name: Palafin
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from the Active Spot to the Bench, you may search your deck for a Palafin ex and switch it with this Pokémon. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon. If you switched a Pokémon in this way, put this card into your deck. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its physical capabilities are no different than a Finizen’s, but when its allies are in danger, it transforms and powers itself up.

Name: Palafin ex
Ability-Effect: Put this Pokémon into play only with the effect of Palafin’s Zero to Hero Ability.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Iron Bundle
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It resembles a mysterious object mentioned in an old book. There are only two reported sightings of this Pokémon.

Name: Walking Wake
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Put up to 9 damage counters on this Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter you placed in this way.
Flavor-Text: This ferocious creature is shrouded in mystery. It’s named after an aquatic monster mentioned in an old expedition journal.

Name: Wellspring Mask Ogerpon ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.

You may shuffle 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your deck. If you do, this attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Zapdos
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.

Name: Shinx
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Flavor-Text: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. Its fur glows when it’s in trouble.

Name: Luxio
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: By gathering their tails together, they collectively generate powerful electricity from their claws.

Name: Luxray ex
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard a card you find there.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Emolga
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: As Emolga flutters through the air, it crackles with electricity. This Pokémon is cute, but it can cause a lot of trouble.

Name: Helioptile
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon’s electric moves.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A now-vanished desert culture treasured these Pokémon. Appropriately, when Heliolisk came to the Galar region, treasure came with them.

Name: Morpeko
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.
Effects: Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It has a small stomach. If it isn’t constantly eating the seeds it keeps in its pockets, it will get hungry immediately.

Name: Tadbulb
Flavor-Text: It floats using the electricity stored in its body. When thunderclouds are around, Tadbulb will float higher off the ground.

Name: Bellibolt
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: What appear to be eyeballs are actually organs for discharging the electricity generated by Bellibolt’s belly-button dynamo.

Name: Wattrel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon make their nests on coastal cliffs. The nests have a strange, crackling texture, and they’re a popular delicacy.

Name: Kilowattrel
Effects: During your next turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses its throat sac to store electricity generated by its wings. There’s hardly any oil in its feathers, so it is a poor swimmer.

Name: Iron Thorns ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, Pokémon with a Rule Box in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities, except for Future Pokémon. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Clefairy
Flavor-Text: On nights with a full moon, they gather together and dance. The surrounding area is enveloped in an abnormal magnetic field.

Name: Clefable
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
Flavor-Text: It has an acute sense of hearing. It can easily hear a pin being dropped nearly 1,100 yards away.

Name: Abra
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may shuffle it and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses its psychic powers while it sleeps. The contents of Abra’s dreams affect the powers that the Pokémon wields.

Name: Kadabra
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using its psychic power, Kadabra levitates as it sleeps. It uses its springy tail as a pillow.

Name: Alakazam
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. You may move any number of damage counters from your opponent’s Pokémon to their other Pokémon in any way you like.
This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.

Name: Girafarig
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Girafarig’s tail has a small head. It instinctively bites at any foe that approaches the Pokémon from behind.

Name: Farigiraf
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Stage 1 Pokémon in play.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The hardened head from the tail protects the head of the main body as Farigiraf whips its long neck around to headbutt enemies.

Name: Chimecho
Effects: Shuffle 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its cries echo inside its hollow body to emerge as beautiful notes for startling and repelling foes.

Name: Flabébé
Effects: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Flabébé rides a red flower. Immediately after birth, this Pokémon begins flying around in search of a flower it likes.

Name: Floette
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses red wavelengths of light to pour its own energy into flowers and draw forth their latent potential.

Name: Florges
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot, and the new Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: They say that flower gardens created by Florges are constantly showered with a power that can heal both body and spirit.

Name: Swirlix
Ability-Effect: If Festival Grounds is in play, this Pokémon may use an attack it has twice. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It eats its own weight in sugar every day. If it doesn’t get enough sugar, it becomes incredibly grumpy.

Name: Slurpuff
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads. If both of them are tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: By taking in a person’s scent, it can sniff out their mental and physical condition. It’s hoped that this skill will have many medical applications.

Name: Sandygast
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
Flavor-Text: It flings sand into the eyes of its prey and tries to close in on them while they’re blinded, but since Sandygast is so slow, the prey tends to escape.

Name: Palossand
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
Flavor-Text: From the hollows in its arms, it fires the bones of its victims, which are all dried up after being drained of their vitality.

Name: Enamorus
Effects: If any of your Pokémon in play are the same type as any of your opponent’s Pokémon in play, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon’s love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

Name: Scream Tail ex
Effects: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Your opponent can’t play any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.
Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Munkidori
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached, you may move up to 3 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Munkidori keeps itself somewhere safe while it toys with its foes, using psychokinesis to induce intense dizziness.

Name: Fezandipiti
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached and is damaged by an attack, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Fezandipiti beats its glossy wings to scatter pheromones that captivate people and Pokémon.

Name: Sandshrew
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: No matter how high a place it falls from, this Pokémon can save itself by rolling into a ball and bouncing.

Name: Sandslash
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The spikes on its body are made up of its hardened hide. It rolls up and attacks foes with its spikes.

Name: Hisuian Growlithe
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.
This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

Name: Hisuian Arcanine
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.

Name: Nosepass
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: When endangered, it may protect itself by increasing its magnetism and drawing nearby iron objects to its body.

Name: Probopass
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Probopass radiates such a powerful magnetic field that nearby electrical appliances are rendered useless while it’s around.

Name: Timburr
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle heavy lumber without difficulty.

Name: Gurdurr
Effects: You may do 30 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch.

Name: Conkeldurr
Effects: This Pokémon is now Confused.
If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, ignore all Energy in this attack’s cost.
Flavor-Text: Rather than rely on their strength, they master moves that make good use of centrifugal force to swing around concrete.

Name: Greninja ex
Effects: You may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Hawlucha live quietly without flocking together. They fight constantly with their natural enemies: noisy groups of Primeape.

Name: Glimmet
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Glimmet’s toxic mineral crystals look just like flower petals. This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder like pollen to protect itself.

Name: Glimmora
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Glimmora’s petals are made of crystallized poison energy. It has recently become evident that these petals resemble Tera Jewels.

Name: Ting-Lu
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack also does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon, and discard that Stadium. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It slowly brings its exceedingly heavy head down upon the ground, splitting the earth open with huge fissures that run over 160 feet deep.

Name: Okidogi
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached, it gets +100 HP, and the attacks it uses do 100 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Okidogi is a ruffian with a short temper. It can pulverize anything by swinging around the chain on its neck.

Name: Cornerstone Mask Ogerpon ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage from attacks done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s Pokémon that have an Ability.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Poochyena
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Poochyena pursue their prey in packs. Once the prey is exhausted, the pack finishes it off. But sometimes the prey retaliates, and the pack flees.

Name: Mightyena
Effects: If Mightyena is on your Bench, this attack does 90 more damage.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Once it’s caught the scent of faraway prey, the leader gives a signal, and the whole pack moves out.

Name: Venipede
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Venipede and Sizzlipede are similar species, but when the two meet, a huge fight ensues.

Name: Whirlipede
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon spins itself rapidly and charges into its opponents. Its top speed is just over 60 mph.

Name: Scolipede
Effects: Put damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.
Flavor-Text: Scolipede latches on to its prey with the claws on its neck before slamming them into the ground and jabbing them with its claws’ toxic spikes.

Name: Brute Bonnet
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
This attack does 50 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is possible that the creature listed as Brute Bonnet in a certain book could actually be this Pokémon.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.
Flavor-Text: People fashion swords from Skarmory’s shed feathers, so this Pokémon is a popular element in heraldic designs.

Name: Aron
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It eats iron ore—and sometimes railroad tracks— to build up the steel armor that protects its body.

Name: Lairon
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Lairon live in mountains brimming with spring water and iron ore, so these Pokémon often came into conflict with humans in the past.

Name: Aggron
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Aggron has a horn sharp enough to perforate thick iron sheets. It brings down its opponents by ramming into them horn first.

Name: Heatran
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.
Effects: Discard all [M] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

Name: Varoom
Effects: Attach a Basic [M] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The steel section is Varoom’s actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.

Name: Revavroom
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Revavroom viciously threatens others with the sound of its exhaust. It sticks its tongue out from its cylindrical mouth and sprays toxic fluids.

Name: Applin
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.

Name: Dipplin
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 70 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.

Name: Dreepy
Flavor-Text: It has a habit of biting at Clauncher even though it doesn’t feed on them. This is said to be vestigial behavior from when Dreepy was alive.

Name: Drakloak
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Put the other card on the bottom of your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon stores up energy in its lungs, then shoots it out. It takes care of Dreepy and battles alongside them until they’re all grown up.

Name: Dragapult ex
Effects: Put 6 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Tatsugiri
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal a Supporter card you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Tatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.

Name: Farfetch’d
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.

Name: Chansey
Effects: Attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It walks carefully to prevent its egg from breaking. However, it is extremely fast at running away.

Name: Blissey ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may move a Basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Effects: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Name: Snorlax
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you do, heal 60 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its stomach’s digestive juices can dissolve any kind of poison. Eating things off the ground doesn’t bother it at all.

Name: Aipom
Flavor-Text: Its tail moves with greater dexterity than its hands. Making deft use of this tail, Aipom lives high among the treetops.

Name: Ambipom
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may flip 2 coins. For each heads, choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.
Flavor-Text: Split into two, the tails are so adept at handling and doing things that Ambipom rarely uses its hands.

Name: Ducklett
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: It strengthens its body by diving into the depths of ponds, swimming around while looking for bog moss to eat.

Name: Swanna
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that many artists have taken inspiration from the sight of a Swanna performing a beautiful dance in the light of dawn.

Name: Bloodmoon Ursaluna ex
Ability-Effect: Blood Moon used by this Pokémon costs [C] less for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Accompanying Flute
Description: Reveal the top 5 cards of your opponent’s deck. You may choose any number of Basic Pokémon you find there and put those Pokémon onto their Bench. Your opponent shuffles the other cards back into their deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Bug Catching Set
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal up to 2 in any combination of [G] Pokémon and Basic [G] Energy cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Caretaker
Description: Draw 2 cards. If you drew any cards in this way and if Community Center is in play, shuffle this Caretaker into your deck instead of discarding it.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Carmine
Description: If you go first, you may use this card during your first turn.Discard your hand and draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Community Center
Description: Once during each player’s turn, if they played a Supporter card from their hand this turn, they may heal 10 damage from each of their Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Cook
Description: Heal 70 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Enhanced Hammer
Description: Discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Festival Grounds
Description: Each Pokémon that has any Energy attached (both yours and your opponent’s) recovers from all Special Conditions and can’t be affected by any Special Conditions.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Handheld Fan
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), move an Energy from the Attacking Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Hassel
Description: You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn.Look at the top 8 cards of your deck and put up to 3 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Hyper Aroma
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Stage 1 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Jamming Tower
Description: Pokémon Tools attached to each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) have no effect.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Kieran
Description: Choose 1:• Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.• During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon ex and Active Pokémon V (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Lana’s Aid
Description: Put up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon that don’t have a Rule Box and Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Love Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon with the same name as 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon in play, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Lucian
Description: Each player shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, each player flips a coin. If heads, that player draws 6 cards. If tails, they draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Lucky Helmet
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), draw 2 cards.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Ogre’s Mask
Description: Choose a Pokémon ex in your discard pile that has “Ogerpon” in its name, and switch it with 1 of your Pokémon ex in play that has “Ogerpon” in its name. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Perrin
Description: Reveal up to 2 Pokémon in your hand and put them into your deck. If you do, search your deck for up to that many Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Raifort
Description: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and discard any number of them. Put the other cards back in any order.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Scoop Up Cyclone
Description: Put 1 of your Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Secret Box
Description: You can use this card only if you discard 3 other cards from your hand.Search your deck for an Item card, a Pokémon Tool card, a Supporter card, and a Stadium card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Survival Brace
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10. Then, discard this card.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Unfair Stamp
Description: You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn.Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 2 cards.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Boomerang Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.If this card is discarded by an effect of an attack used by the Pokémon this card is attached to, attach this card from your discard pile to that Pokémon after attacking.

Name: Legacy Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card. This effect of your Legacy Energy can’t be applied more than once per game.

Name: Pinsir
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. At the end of your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

Name: Sunflora
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each [R] Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Since it converts sunlight into energy, it is always looking in the direction of the sun.

Name: Dipplin
Ability-Effect: If Festival Grounds is in play, this Pokémon may use an attack it has twice. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.

Name: Poltchageist
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Poltchageist looks like a regional form of Sinistea, but it was recently discovered that the two Pokémon are entirely unrelated.

Name: Torkoal
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Coal is the source of Torkoal’s energy. Large amounts of coal can be found in the mountains where they live.

Name: Infernape
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [R] Energy card, a Basic [F] Energy card, or 1 of each from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its crown of fire is indicative of its fiery nature. It is beaten by none in terms of quickness.

Name: Froslass
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, put 1 damage counter on each Pokémon that has an Ability (both yours and your opponent’s), except any Froslass.
Flavor-Text: It freezes prey by blowing its −58 degree Fahrenheit breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.

Name: Phione
Effects: Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the water warms, they inflate the flotation sac on their heads and drift languidly on the sea in packs.

Name: Cramorant
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It’s an incredibly poor learner because it uses the bare minimum amount of energy for its brain. It focuses on battling instead.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A now-vanished desert culture treasured these Pokémon. Appropriately, when Heliolisk came to the Galar region, treasure came with them.

Name: Wattrel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon make their nests on coastal cliffs. The nests have a strange, crackling texture, and they’re a popular delicacy.

Name: Chimecho
Effects: Shuffle 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its cries echo inside its hollow body to emerge as beautiful notes for startling and repelling foes.

Name: Enamorus
Effects: If any of your Pokémon in play are the same type as any of your opponent’s Pokémon in play, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon’s love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

Name: Hisuian Growlithe
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.
This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

Name: Probopass
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Probopass radiates such a powerful magnetic field that nearby electrical appliances are rendered useless while it’s around.

Name: Timburr
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle heavy lumber without difficulty.

Name: Lairon
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Lairon live in mountains brimming with spring water and iron ore, so these Pokémon often came into conflict with humans in the past.

Name: Applin
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.

Name: Tatsugiri
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal a Supporter card you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Tatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.

Name: Chansey
Effects: Attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It walks carefully to prevent its egg from breaking. However, it is extremely fast at running away.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Name: Sinistcha ex
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each Basic [G] Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those Energy cards into your deck.
Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Teal Mask Ogerpon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [G] Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Magcargo ex
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard the top card of each player’s deck. This attack does 140 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Hearthflame Mask Ogerpon ex
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage, and discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Palafin ex
Ability-Effect: Put this Pokémon into play only with the effect of Palafin’s Zero to Hero Ability.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Wellspring Mask Ogerpon ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.

You may shuffle 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your deck. If you do, this attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Luxray ex
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard a card you find there.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Iron Thorns ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, Pokémon with a Rule Box in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities, except for Future Pokémon. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Scream Tail ex
Effects: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Your opponent can’t play any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.
Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Greninja ex
Effects: You may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Put 6 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
